- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". - loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

1	"""V30 Phase-2 sweep wired into the JobRunner abstraction.
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md (Phase B M-9): launch the existing V30 Phase-2 sweep
4	as a subprocess, monitor run_log.txt for phase boundaries, emit
5	checkpoints at each boundary so pause/cancel work cooperatively.
6	
7	Design:
8	- Subprocess approach (NOT in-process refactor) because the sweep is
9	  ~5000 lines of asyncio orchestration and refactoring it phase-by-phase
10	  is a Phase C task.
11	- Phase boundaries derived from run_log.txt timestamps (the sweep
12	  already writes one line per phase). The runner tails the log and
13	  emits control.checkpoint() per phase line.
14	- Cancel: subprocess gets SIGTERM (or terminate() on Windows). Caller
15	  waits up to 30s for graceful shutdown then SIGKILL.
16	- Pause: NOT supported for V30 in Phase B. The sweep has no clean
17	  mid-sweep pause point. JobControl.Paused raises if pause is requested,
18	  which the worker converts to mark_paused — but the resume path will
19	  re-run from scratch (no incremental checkpoint state inside V30).
20	  Phase C M-13 progressive surfaces enable real pause via SSE streaming.
21	
22	Codex M-9 anticipates:
23	- Subprocess env passes OPENROUTER_API_KEY etc through to the child
24	- Python interpreter resolution is sys.executable (matches parent venv)
25	- Per-job artifact dir: out_root/<domain>/<slug>/ — resolves via the
26	  manifest_path field returned in checkpoint state
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import re
34	import signal
35	import subprocess
36	import sys
37	import threading
38	import time
39	from dataclasses import dataclass
40	from pathlib import Path
41	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
42	
43	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.job_queue import Job
44	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.job_runner import JobControl, JobRunner
45	from src.polaris_graph.audit_ir.progress_surfaces import (
46	    SurfaceKind,
47	    get_surface_bus,
48	)
49	
50	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
51	
52	
53	# Codex M-13: map V30 phase keys to progressive Inspector surfaces.
54	# Per FINAL_PLAN's t-table:
55	#   t=0      pre-flight estimate          → PREFLIGHT
56	#   t=0-2    upload/parse progress        → PARSE_PROGRESS
57	#   t=2-15   live source discovery + tier → TIER_MIX
58	#   t=15-45  frame coverage manifest      → FRAME_COVERAGE
59	#   t=45-90  contradiction queue          → CONTRADICTION_QUEUE
60	#   t=90-120 first verified claim cards   → VERIFIED_CLAIM
61	#   t=120-145 final synthesis             → SYNTHESIS_COMPLETE
62	#
63	# Map each V30 phase to whichever surface it represents. Phases
64	# that don't represent a user-visible milestone map to None.
65	_PHASE_TO_SURFACE: dict[str, SurfaceKind | None] = {
66	    # Codex M-13 v2 review fix: do NOT remap "scope" to PREFLIGHT.
67	    # The runner emits PREFLIGHT explicitly at t=0 with stable
68	    # estimate fields (estimated_minutes, cost_cap_usd). If "scope"
69	    # later overwrote that snapshot, late SSE replayers would lose
70	    # the estimate. "scope" is a no-op for surface emission; the
71	    # progress bar still moves via control.checkpoint.
72	    "scope": None,
73	    "retrieval": SurfaceKind.TIER_MIX,
74	    "corpus": SurfaceKind.TIER_MIX,
75	    "adequacy": SurfaceKind.FRAME_COVERAGE,
76	    "completeness": SurfaceKind.FRAME_COVERAGE,
77	    "contradict": SurfaceKind.CONTRADICTION_QUEUE,
78	    "select": SurfaceKind.VERIFIED_CLAIM,
79	    "generation": SurfaceKind.VERIFIED_CLAIM,
80	    "v30_phase2": SurfaceKind.VERIFIED_CLAIM,
81	    "evaluator": SurfaceKind.VERIFIED_CLAIM,
82	    "judge": SurfaceKind.VERIFIED_CLAIM,
83	    "eval_gate": SurfaceKind.VERIFIED_CLAIM,
84	    "v30_phase1": SurfaceKind.SYNTHESIS_COMPLETE,
85	    "cost": SurfaceKind.SYNTHESIS_COMPLETE,
86	    "status": SurfaceKind.SYNTHESIS_COMPLETE,
87	}
88	
89	
90	# Canonical phase milestones the V30 sweep emits to run_log.txt.
91	#
92	# Codex M-9 review fix: order + percentages now reflect the ACTUAL run-14
93	# emission order, not the imagined one. Run-14 log shows:
94	#   [scope] -> [M-28] -> [M-35] -> [retrieval] -> [m48] -> [corpus]
95	#   -> [adequacy] -> [completeness] -> [contradict] -> [select]
96	#   -> [generation] -> [V30-P2] (3 lines) -> [m44/m47/m53]
97	#   -> [evaluator] -> [judge] -> [eval_gate] -> [V30] (5 lines)
98	#   -> [cost] -> [status]
99	V30_PHASES = (
100	    ("scope",              5.0,   "Scope gate"),
101	    ("retrieval",          50.0,  "Live retrieval (corpus assembly)"),
102	    ("corpus",             55.0,  "Corpus tier classification"),
103	    ("adequacy",           58.0,  "Corpus adequacy gate"),
104	    ("completeness",       60.0,  "Topic completeness check"),
105	    ("contradict",         62.0,  "Contradiction detection"),
106	    ("select",             65.0,  "Evidence selection"),
107	    ("generation",         75.0,  "Multi-section generation"),
108	    ("v30_phase2",         85.0,  "V30 Phase-2 slot-bound generation"),
109	    ("evaluator",          90.0,  "Evaluator rule checks"),
110	    ("judge",              92.0,  "Judge verdicts"),
111	    ("eval_gate",          94.0,  "Evaluator gate decision"),
112	    ("v30_phase1",         97.0,  "V30 Phase-1 frame coverage"),
113	    ("cost",               99.0,  "Cost ledger finalized"),
114	    ("status",             100.0, "Sweep complete"),
115	)

119	# substring matches on log lines.

121	# Codex M-9 review fix: patterns now match the actual canonical bracketed
122	# tags emitted by run_honest_sweep_r3.py. Order is checked sequentially
123	# (first match wins) so more specific tags come first.
124	_PHASE_PATTERNS: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
125	    # Terminal markers checked first.
126	    ("status",       "[status]"),
127	    ("cost",         "[cost]"),
128	    # V30 Phase-1 (post-eval-gate) and Phase-2 (during generation) are
129	    # tagged distinctly: [V30] vs [V30-P2].
130	    ("v30_phase1",   "[v30]"),
131	    ("v30_phase2",   "[v30-p2]"),
132	    # Eval pipeline.
133	    ("eval_gate",    "[eval_gate]"),
134	    ("judge",        "[judge]"),
135	    ("evaluator",    "[evaluator]"),
136	    # Generation + selection.
137	    ("select",       "[select]"),
138	    ("generation",   "[generation]"),
139	    # Pre-generation gates.
140	    ("contradict",   "[contradict]"),
141	    ("completeness", "[completeness]"),
142	    ("adequacy",     "[adequacy]"),
143	    ("corpus",       "[corpus]"),
144	    ("retrieval",    "[retrieval]"),
145	    ("scope",        "[scope]"),
146	)
147	
148	_PHASE_PCT: dict[str, float] = {key: pct for key, pct, _ in V30_PHASES}
149	_PHASE_MSG: dict[str, str] = {key: msg for key, _, msg in V30_PHASES}
150	
151	
152	def _read_env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
153	    """Codex M-13: env-driven numeric estimate for the PREFLIGHT
154	    surface. Garbage values fall back to default per LAW VI."""
155	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
156	    if raw is None:
157	        return default
158	    try:
159	        return float(raw)
160	    except ValueError:
161	        return default
162	
163	
164	@dataclass
165	class HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig:
166	    """Runtime configuration for the V30 runner."""
167	
168	    repo_root: Path
169	    sweep_script: Path
170	    out_root: Path
171	    python_bin: str = sys.executable
172	    poll_interval_s: float = 1.0
173	    cancel_grace_s: float = 30.0
174	    extra_env: Mapping[str, str] | None = None
175	
176	
177	class HonestSweepJobRunner(JobRunner):
178	    """Runs the V30 Phase-2 sweep as a subprocess, checkpoints per phase.
179	
180	    Job params (required): {"slug": "<canonical_slug>"}
181	    Job params (optional): {"domain": "<domain>"} — defaults derived
182	                                                    from slug map.
183	
184	    Returns: artifact_dir path on success.
185	    """
186	
187	    template_id = "v30_clinical"
188	
189	    def __init__(self, config: HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig) -> None:
190	        self._config = config
191	
192	    def run(self, job: Job, control: JobControl) -> str | None:
193	        slug = (job.params or {}).get("slug")
194	        if not slug:
195	            raise ValueError("v30_clinical: job.params.slug is required")
196	        cfg = self._config
197	        if not cfg.sweep_script.exists():
198	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"Sweep script missing: {cfg.sweep_script}")
199	
200	        # Codex M-9 review fix: per-job output root so concurrent or
201	        # sequential reruns of the same slug never overwrite each other.
202	        # Layout becomes out_root/<job_id>/<domain>/<slug>/.
203	        per_job_out_root = cfg.out_root / job.job_id
204	        per_job_out_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
205	
206	        # Subprocess env: inherit + add V30 env extras.
207	        env = dict(os.environ)
208	        if cfg.extra_env:
209	            env.update(cfg.extra_env)
210	
211	        cmd = [
212	            cfg.python_bin,
213	            str(cfg.sweep_script),
214	            "--only", slug,
215	            "--out-root", str(per_job_out_root),
216	        ]
217	
218	        # Initial checkpoint so the queue shows progress immediately.
219	        # Codex M-9 v2 review fix: guard this with the same Paused →
220	        # RuntimeError conversion as the loop checkpoints.
221	        try:
222	            control.checkpoint(
223	                progress_pct=1.0,
224	                message="Launching V30 sweep subprocess",
225	                state={"slug": slug, "phase": "launch"},
226	            )
227	        except JobControl.Cancelled:
228	            raise
229	        except JobControl.Paused:
230	            raise RuntimeError(
231	                "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
232	                "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead."
233	            ) from None
234	
235	        # Codex M-13: emit a PREFLIGHT surface so the Inspector UI
236	        # can render scope/cost/time estimate immediately at t=0.
237	        # The actual estimates are derived from the slug + sweep
238	        # config; richer per-slug estimates land in Phase C.
239	        self._emit_surface(
240	            job, SurfaceKind.PREFLIGHT, {
241	                "slug": slug,
242	                "estimated_minutes": 145,  # FINAL_PLAN p90 target
243	                "cost_cap_usd": _read_env_float("PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN", 10.0),
244	                "phase": "launch",
245	            },
246	        )
247	
248	        logger.info("HonestSweepJobRunner: launching %s", " ".join(cmd))
249	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
250	            cmd,
251	            cwd=str(cfg.repo_root),
252	            env=env,
253	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
254	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
255	            text=True,
256	        )
257	
258	        seen_phases: set[str] = set()
259	        last_phase = "launch"
260	        last_pct = 1.0
261	        # Tail the subprocess stdout in a thread so we can poll the
262	        # control surface in the main thread.
263	        log_lines: list[str] = []
264	        log_lock = threading.Lock()
265	
266	        def _drain() -> None:
267	            assert proc.stdout is not None
268	            for line in proc.stdout:
269	                with log_lock:
270	                    log_lines.append(line)
271	
272	        drain_thread = threading.Thread(target=_drain, daemon=True, name="v30-stdout-drain")
273	        drain_thread.start()
274	
275	        # Codex M-9 v3 review fix: subprocess termination MUST happen
276	        # in `finally`, not in individual `except` blocks. Earlier
277	        # iterations called `_terminate_subprocess` only inside
278	        # `except JobControl.Cancelled` / `except JobControl.Paused`.
279	        # That left a hole: when the inner per-phase or periodic
280	        # checkpoint converted Paused → RuntimeError, the RuntimeError
281	        # propagated past the outer Cancelled/Paused excepts (no match)
282	        # and the V30 child kept running in the background while the
283	        # job was marked failed. Centralizing termination in `finally`
284	        # closes that gap for ALL exit paths (Cancelled, Paused,
285	        # Paused→RuntimeError, any other RuntimeError, normal exit).
286	        # `_terminate_subprocess` is idempotent — it no-ops if the
287	        # process has already exited (proc.poll() is not None).
288	        try:
289	            while True:
290	                # Detect new phase boundaries from the latest log lines.
291	                with log_lock:
292	                    snapshot = list(log_lines)
293	                    log_lines.clear()
294	                for line in snapshot:
295	                    phase = self._classify_phase(line)
296	                    if phase and phase not in seen_phases:
297	                        seen_phases.add(phase)
298	                        last_phase = phase
299	                        last_pct = _PHASE_PCT[phase]

301	                            control.checkpoint(
302	                                progress_pct=last_pct,
303	                                message=_PHASE_MSG[phase],
304	                                state={

306	                                    "phase": phase,
307	                                    "log_line": line.strip()[:300],
308	                                },
309	                            )
310	                        except JobControl.Cancelled:
311	                            raise
312	                        except JobControl.Paused:
313	                            raise RuntimeError(
314	                                "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
315	                                "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead."
316	                            ) from None
317	                        # Codex M-13: emit a progressive surface
318	                        # for the new phase. Phases that don't map
319	                        # to a user-visible milestone (PHASE_MAP
320	                        # entry is None) are skipped. The progress
321	                        # bar still moves via control.checkpoint
322	                        # above; surfaces are an additional view.
323	                        surface_kind = _PHASE_TO_SURFACE.get(phase)
324	                        if surface_kind is not None:
325	                            self._emit_surface(
326	                                job, surface_kind, {
327	                                    "slug": slug,
328	                                    "phase": phase,
329	                                    "progress_pct": last_pct,
330	                                    "message": _PHASE_MSG[phase],
331	                                    "log_line": line.strip()[:300],
332	                                },
333	                            )
334	
335	                # Periodic checkpoint even when no new phase fires
336	                # (so cancel/pause requests are detected within
337	                # poll_interval_s).
338	                try:
339	                    control.checkpoint(
340	                        progress_pct=last_pct,
341	                        message=_PHASE_MSG.get(last_phase, last_phase),
342	                        state={"slug": slug, "phase": last_phase},
343	                    )
344	                except JobControl.Cancelled:
345	                    raise
346	                except JobControl.Paused:
347	                    # Codex M-9 review fix: pause is not supported for
348	                    # V30 in Phase B. Convert to a hard failure so the
349	                    # user sees "Pause unsupported for v30_clinical"
350	                    # rather than ending up in a paused/resumable state
351	                    # that re-runs from scratch on resume.
352	                    raise RuntimeError(
353	                        "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
354	                        "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead. "
355	                        "(Phase C M-13 progressive surfaces will enable "
356	                        "real pause via SSE streaming.)"
357	                    ) from None
358	
359	                # Subprocess done?
360	                rc = proc.poll()
361	                if rc is not None:
362	                    break
363	                time.sleep(cfg.poll_interval_s)
364	
365	        except JobControl.Paused:
366	            # Safety net: if Paused escapes through to the outer scope
367	            # (neither inner except converted it), convert here.
368	            raise RuntimeError(
369	                "Pause is not supported for template_id='v30_clinical' "
370	                "in Phase B. Use cancel + re-enqueue instead."
371	            ) from None
372	        finally:
373	            self._terminate_subprocess(proc, cfg.cancel_grace_s)
374	            drain_thread.join(timeout=2.0)
375	
376	        if proc.returncode != 0:
377	            tail = "".join(log_lines[-50:]) if log_lines else ""
378	            raise RuntimeError(
379	                f"V30 sweep failed (rc={proc.returncode}). Tail:\n{tail}"
380	            )
381	
382	        # Resolve the artifact dir. Per-job out_root means there's
383	        # exactly one valid <domain>/<slug>/ underneath; no mtime
384	        # tie-breaking needed.
385	        artifact_dir = self._resolve_artifact_dir(per_job_out_root, slug)
386	        return str(artifact_dir) if artifact_dir else None

389	    # Helpers
390	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
391	
392	    @staticmethod
393	    def _emit_surface(
394	        job: Job, kind: SurfaceKind, payload: dict[str, Any]
395	    ) -> None:
396	        """Codex M-13: best-effort surface emission. Never let a
397	        bus error fail the audit run — surfaces are auxiliary."""
398	        try:
399	            get_surface_bus().emit(job.job_id, kind, payload)
400	        except Exception:  # pragma: no cover - defensive
401	            logger.exception("surface emit failed for job %s", job.job_id)
402	
403	    @staticmethod
404	    def _classify_phase(line: str) -> str | None:
405	        lc = line.lower()
406	        for phase_key, pattern in _PHASE_PATTERNS:
407	            if pattern.lower() in lc:
408	                return phase_key
409	        return None
410	
411	    @staticmethod
412	    def _terminate_subprocess(proc: subprocess.Popen, grace_s: float) -> None:
413	        if proc.poll() is not None:
414	            return
415	        try:
416	            proc.terminate()  # SIGTERM on POSIX, TerminateProcess on Windows
417	        except Exception:
418	            pass
419	        try:
420	            proc.wait(timeout=grace_s)
421	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
422	            try:
423	                proc.kill()
424	                proc.wait(timeout=5.0)
425	            except Exception:
426	                pass
427	
428	    @staticmethod
429	    def _resolve_artifact_dir(out_root: Path, slug: str) -> Path | None:
430	        """Find the artifact directory for the given slug.
431	
432	        Layout: out_root/<domain>/<slug>/. We glob for the slug match
433	        and return the most-recently-modified candidate.
434	        """
435	        if not out_root.is_dir():
436	            return None
437	        candidates: list[Path] = []
438	        for domain_dir in out_root.iterdir():
439	            if not domain_dir.is_dir():
440	                continue
441	            slug_dir = domain_dir / slug
442	            if slug_dir.is_dir() and (slug_dir / "manifest.json").exists():
443	                candidates.append(slug_dir)
444	        if not candidates:
445	            return None
446	        candidates.sort(key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
447	        return candidates[0]

452	    if repo_root is None:
453	        # repo_root = src/polaris_graph/audit_ir/honest_sweep_job_runner.py.parents[3]
454	        repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
455	    config = HonestSweepJobRunnerConfig(

457	        sweep_script=repo_root / "scripts" / "run_full_scale_v30_phase2.py",
458	        out_root=repo_root / "outputs" / "polaris_v30_jobs",
459	    )
460	    return HonestSweepJobRunner(config)

1	"""GH I-deepfix-003 (#1374) — junk-deletion grounding gate.
2	
3	Operator rule change 2026-07-09 (CLAUDE.md §-1.3.1): the §-1.3 never-DROP rule is
4	LIFTED for genuine JUNK. Weighting junk to zero is insufficient — a zero-weight row
5	still sits in the evidence pool, the bibliography, and the per-claim corroboration
6	counts, where it pollutes a clinical report (a bot-detection page anchoring a claim; a
7	predatory PDF padding the count; an off-topic law/tourism source). Two classes are
8	hard-DELETED from the run-level grounding pool BEFORE generation:
9	
10	  (a) CHROME NON-SOURCES — bot/captcha/cookie/404/login/empty pages. A failed fetch is
11	      not a source. The content-integrity detector stamps ``row["content_integrity_junk"]``
12	      upstream (the re-tier seam); this gate deletes the stamped rows.
13	  (b) SEMANTICALLY-CONFIRMED OFF-TOPIC whole sources — a source a SEMANTIC topic judge
14	      confirmed off-topic to the research question. DEFAULT path (Fix 1,
15	      ``is_row_deletable_offtopic``) deletes ONLY on the topic judge's affirmative
16	      OFF_SUBJECT stamp: a weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label``
17	      (``demoted`` / ``escalated_demoted``) can NEVER delete, and a positive relevance
18	      verdict (``relevant`` / ``escalated_relevant``) vetoes deletion UNCONDITIONALLY. Judge
19	      verdict ONLY, FAIL-OPEN (any uncertainty / missing verdict / error => KEEP), never a
20	      lexical/tier/number rule. The legacy weight-label reuse
21	      (``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` via ``is_row_confirmed_offtopic``) is
22	      reachable only behind ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY=0`` (byte-identical OFF).
23	
24	STILL BINDING (§-1.3): credible ON-TOPIC sources, even low-tier / social / non-journal,
25	are NEVER deleted here (they are neither content-junk nor confirmed-off-topic). An
26	off-topic SPAN inside an on-topic source is handled at compose (the span screen), not
27	here. Duplicates consolidate elsewhere. The faithfulness engine is UNTOUCHED — deleting a
28	row can only SHRINK the grounding pool, so a claim citing a deleted junk row FAILS
29	strict_verify (it can never make one PASS). A deleted junk row was never a valid anchor.
30	
31	MARQUEE/CONTRACT EXEMPTION: a required-entity / contract-bound anchor is on-topic by
32	construction and is NEVER deleted (its evidence_id is in ``exempt_ids``), mirroring the
33	topic gate's existing anchor exemption.
34	
35	Deletion is DISCLOSED: every deleted row is RETURNED with its reason for the manifest + a
36	LOUD log + a Methods line (fail loud, never silent — LAW II).
37	
38	Pure leaf module: no network, no model import, no faithfulness-engine import. Two env
39	kill-switches (LAW VI), DEFAULT ON. When the corpus has zero junk / off-topic rows the
40	groundable pool is byte-identical to the input.
41	"""
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import logging
45	import os
46	from typing import Any, Iterable, Mapping
47	
48	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.junk_deletion_gate")
49	
50	_CHROME_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_CHROME_NONSOURCE"
51	_OFFTOPIC_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_SOURCE"
52	# Fix 1 (I-deepfix-003 over-deletion): the off-topic DELETE fires ONLY on the topic judge's
53	# affirmative OFF_SUBJECT stamp; a weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label`` can NEVER
54	# delete. DEFAULT ON. OFF => byte-identical legacy weight-label path.
55	_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY"
56	# Fix 2 (I-deepfix-003 over-deletion): the off-topic DELETE fires ONLY on an OFF_SUBJECT
57	# stamp THIS run's topic judge produced. A STALE stamp reloaded from an earlier run's
58	# corpus_snapshot (the judge did NOT re-run this pass) demote-KEEPs. DEFAULT ON. OFF =>
59	# freshness is NOT enforced (byte-identical to the Fix-1-only path: any OFF_SUBJECT stamp
60	# deletes regardless of provenance).
61	_FRESH_VERDICT_FLAG = "PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY"
62	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", ""})
63	
64	# An affirmative positive-relevance verdict from the content-relevance judge. A row carrying
65	# one is on-topic by the judge's own word and VETOES deletion unconditionally.
66	_POSITIVE_RELEVANCE_LABELS = frozenset({"relevant", "escalated_relevant"})

68	# split). ``topic_off_subject`` is normally the bool True (Fix 3 sidecar); these tolerate a
69	# string representation without ever matching OFF_ASPECT or a weight label.
70	_OFF_SUBJECT_TRUE_TOKENS = frozenset({"off_subject", "offsubject", "true", "yes", "on", "1"})

74	    """Kill-switch for the chrome-non-source deletion. DEFAULT ON. Falsey =>

76	    return os.getenv(_CHROME_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

102	    return os.getenv(_FRESH_VERDICT_FLAG, "1").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

104	
105	def is_row_content_junk(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
106	    """True iff the content-integrity detector stamped this row as a chrome non-source
107	    (bot / cookie / 404 / login / empty). FAIL-OPEN: any error / missing / falsey flag
108	    => NOT junk (row KEPT). A predicate bug must NEVER delete a source."""
109	    try:
110	        v = row.get("content_integrity_junk")
111	        return bool(v) and str(v).strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES
112	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never delete on a predicate bug
113	        logger.warning(
114	            "[junk_deletion_gate] content-junk predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "
115	            "(fail-open).", str(exc)[:160],
116	        )
117	        return False
118	
119	
120	def is_row_confirmed_offtopic(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
121	    """True iff a SEMANTIC topic judge confirmed this WHOLE source off-topic to the
122	    research question. Reuses ``weighted_enrichment._is_confirmed_offtopic`` — the single
123	    source of truth the compose off-topic screen also uses — so the gate and the screen
124	    can never disagree. FAIL-OPEN: an unjudged / ``relevant`` / ``escalated_relevant`` /
125	    missing-verdict / error row is NOT off-topic (row KEPT)."""
126	    try:
127	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.weighted_enrichment import (  # noqa: PLC0415
128	            _is_confirmed_offtopic,
129	        )
130	        return bool(_is_confirmed_offtopic(row))
131	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — never delete on a predicate bug
132	        logger.warning(
133	            "[junk_deletion_gate] offtopic predicate errored (%s) — row KEPT "
134	            "(fail-open).", str(exc)[:160],
135	        )
136	        return False

139	def _stamped_off_subject(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> bool:
140	    """True iff the topic judge stamped this WHOLE source OFF_SUBJECT — a clearly different
141	    subject (the DELETABLE class of the OFF split, e.g. a scholar-mill / tourism / religion
142	    page in an AI-labor report). The Fix-3 sidecar is the bool ``topic_off_subject=True``; a
143	    string verdict ``topic_relevance_verdict == "OFF_SUBJECT"`` is tolerated as the same
144	    signal. Legacy ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` alone (which conflates OFF_ASPECT 'same entity,
145	    wrong aspect' with OFF_SUBJECT) does NOT count — a demote-keep aspect verdict is never a
146	    delete trigger, so only the explicit subject-level stamp deletes."""
147	    v = row.get("topic_off_subject")
148	    if v is True:
149	        return True
150	    if isinstance(v, str) and v.strip().lower() in _OFF_SUBJECT_TRUE_TOKENS:
151	        return True
152	    verdict = str(row.get("topic_relevance_verdict", "") or "").strip().lower()
153	    return verdict == "off_subject"

155	
156	def is_row_deletable_offtopic(
157	    row: Mapping[str, Any],
158	    fresh_off_subject_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
159	) -> bool:
160	    """DELETE predicate for the confirmed-off-topic class (topic-judge-only path, Fix 1 + Fix 2).
161	
162	    Returns True (row is DELETABLE) iff the topic judge AFFIRMATIVELY stamped this WHOLE
163	    source OFF_SUBJECT. Three hard guarantees keep this from over-deleting:
164	
165	    * A weight/reranker ``content_relevance_label`` (``demoted`` / ``escalated_demoted``) is
166	      NEVER a delete trigger — those are weight-demote-to-0.25 KEEP labels (a Qwen3-Reranker
167	      numeric score / a GLM 'INSUFFICIENT' entailment), NOT topic verdicts. Reusing them as a
168	      DELETE trigger is exactly what hard-deleted 197 on-topic rows (St. Louis Fed, OECD, ILO,
169	      McKinsey, HBS, Wikipedia, 23 T1 journal papers) in the I-deepfix-003 pass.
170	    * An affirmative positive-relevance verdict (``content_relevance_label`` in
171	      {relevant, escalated_relevant}) VETOES deletion UNCONDITIONALLY — even against a
172	      stale/false OFF stamp. When the two judges disagree, the positive relevance wins.
173	    * Fix 2 (fresh-verdict-only): when ``fresh_off_subject_ids`` is supplied (a concrete set —
174	      the evidence_ids THIS run's topic judge freshly confirmed OFF_SUBJECT) AND
175	      ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_FRESH_VERDICT_ONLY`` is ON, a row whose OFF_SUBJECT stamp is NOT in

205	def partition_rows(
206	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
207	    exempt_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
208	    fresh_off_subject_ids: "Iterable[str] | None" = None,
209	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
210	    """Split the run-level grounding pool (``evidence_for_gen``) into
211	    ``(kept, deleted)``.
212	
213	    A row is DELETED iff it is a chrome non-source (kill-switch A) OR a
214	    confirmed-off-topic source (kill-switch B), AND its ``evidence_id`` is NOT in
215	    ``exempt_ids`` (a marquee / contract-bound anchor is on-topic by construction and is
216	    never deleted). Each deleted row carries a ``deletion_reason`` for disclosure.
217	
218	    Order-preserving; never mutates the input rows (a deleted row is copied before the
219	    reason is stamped); idempotent (re-running on a clean pool returns it unchanged with
220	    an empty deleted list). When BOTH kill-switches are OFF, returns ``(list(rows), [])``
221	    — byte-identical.
222	    """
223	    chrome_on = chrome_deletion_enabled()
224	    offtopic_on = offtopic_deletion_enabled()
225	    if not chrome_on and not offtopic_on:
226	        return list(rows), []
227	    exempt = {str(e) for e in (exempt_ids or ())}
228	    # Fix 2: materialize the fresh OFF_SUBJECT id set ONCE (or None to leave freshness
229	    # un-enforced). A stale-stamp row whose id is absent demote-KEEPs (see
230	    # is_row_deletable_offtopic).
231	    fresh_ids = None if fresh_off_subject_ids is None else {str(e) for e in fresh_off_subject_ids}
232	    kept: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
233	    deleted: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
234	    for row in rows:
235	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
236	            kept.append(row)
237	            continue
238	        eid = str(row.get("evidence_id", "") or "")
239	        if eid and eid in exempt:
240	            kept.append(row)  # marquee / contract anchor — NEVER deleted
241	            continue
242	        reason = ""
243	        if chrome_on and is_row_content_junk(row):
244	            reason = "content_integrity_junk:" + str(
245	                row.get("content_integrity_class", "chrome") or "chrome"
246	            )
247	        elif offtopic_on:
248	            # Fix 1: default-ON path deletes ONLY on the topic judge's OFF_SUBJECT stamp
249	            # (weight labels can never delete; positive relevance vetoes). The legacy
250	            # weight-label predicate stays reachable ONLY behind the OFF kill-switch so
251	            # ``PG_DELETE_OFFTOPIC_TOPIC_JUDGE_ONLY=0`` is byte-identical to pre-Fix-1.
252	            if topic_judge_only_deletion_enabled():
253	                if is_row_deletable_offtopic(row, fresh_off_subject_ids=fresh_ids):
254	                    reason = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
255	            elif is_row_confirmed_offtopic(row):
256	                reason = "confirmed_offtopic"
257	        if reason:
258	            copied = dict(row)
259	            copied["deletion_reason"] = reason
260	            deleted.append(copied)
261	        else:
262	            kept.append(row)
263	    return kept, deleted

266	def _deletion_signal(reason: str) -> str:
267	    """Human-readable 'which signal fired' derived from the deletion_reason — a chrome class
268	    or the topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT stamp. Keeps the disclosure honest about the exact
269	    category (chrome non-source vs confirmed-off-topic) that removed each row."""
270	    if reason.startswith("content_integrity_junk"):
271	        cls = reason.split(":", 1)[1] if ":" in reason else "chrome"
272	        return "chrome:" + (cls or "chrome")
273	    if reason.startswith("confirmed_offtopic"):
274	        return "topic_judge_off_subject"
275	    return reason or "unknown"
276	
277	
278	def _judge_verdict_summary(row: Mapping[str, Any]) -> str:
279	    """Compact per-source verdict trail the row carried at deletion time (read-only — never a
280	    delete trigger here). Surfaces WHY the source was judged, so a reviewer can second-guess
281	    any deletion from the manifest alone."""
282	    parts: list[str] = []
283	    if _stamped_off_subject(row):
284	        parts.append("topic=OFF_SUBJECT")
285	    if row.get("topic_offtopic_demoted") is True:
286	        parts.append("topic_offtopic_demoted=True")
287	    label = str(row.get("content_relevance_label", "") or "").strip()
288	    if label:
289	        parts.append("content_relevance_label=" + label)
290	    verdict = str(row.get("topic_relevance_verdict", "") or "").strip()
291	    if verdict:
292	        parts.append("topic_relevance_verdict=" + verdict)
293	    ci_class = str(row.get("content_integrity_class", "") or "").strip()
294	    if ci_class:
295	        parts.append("content_integrity_class=" + ci_class)
296	    return ";".join(parts)
297	
298	
299	def disclosure_records(deleted_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
300	    """Compact disclosure rows for the run telemetry — the audit trail that proves the
301	    pipeline found and DELETED each junk / off-topic source (recorded, never silent).
302	
303	    Fix 5 (records half): each record now carries the per-source SIGNAL that fired
304	    (chrome class vs topic-judge OFF_SUBJECT), the JUDGE VERDICT trail, and the source TIER
305	    alongside the existing evidence_id / title / url / deletion_reason — enough to second-
306	    guess any single deletion straight from the manifest (fail-loud, never silent)."""
307	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
308	    for row in deleted_rows:
309	        if not isinstance(row, Mapping):
310	            continue
311	        reason = str(row.get("deletion_reason", "") or "")
312	        out.append({
313	            "evidence_id": row.get("evidence_id", ""),
314	            "title": row.get("source_title") or row.get("title") or "",
315	            "url": row.get("url") or row.get("source_url") or "",
316	            "tier": row.get("tier") or row.get("source_tier") or "",
317	            "deletion_reason": reason,
318	            "signal": _deletion_signal(reason),
319	            "judge_verdict": _judge_verdict_summary(row),
320	            "excluded_from_grounding": True,
321	        })
322	    return out
